Mount ₣BB / ₢BBAAM. The five-step depot-identity-collapse arc landed across this session — d2dec554 is the head; tree clean. Rust
   + bash both green at build/unit/fast tier. Now exercising end-to-end against real GCP.

  Read first, in order:
  - .claude/jjm/jjp_uBuB.md (paddock — release-branch contract + seven depot-identity-collapse design locks; read the
  Release-branch execution contract paragraph specifically)
  - git log --oneline ee816373..HEAD (the five Step commits + cleanup)
  - Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh (zrbgp_depot_state_emit, zrbgp_depot_list_update, rbgp_depot_levy, rbgp_depot_unmake, rbgp_depot_list)
  - Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrp_pristine.rs (cases 2-3 + rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker + rbtdrp_compose_project_id)

  Goal: run the pristine-lifecycle fixture against live GCP. This creates two real depot projects (one per case 2 + case 3) with
  associated GAR repo / GCS bucket / Cloud Build pools / Mason SA each, then soft-deletes them at the end. Real cloud spend,
  ~minutes per case.

  Operator-facing prerequisite chain (per paddock — rbw-tP is THE release gate):
  1. Confirm Payor OAuth is healthy (refresh if needed: tt/rbw-gPR.PayorRefresh.sh).
  2. Marshal-zero the local regime: tt/rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh
  3. Commit the marshal-zero state (the commit signature is what case 1 attestation checks).
  4. Run the fixture: tt/rbtd-r.Run.pristine-lifecycle.sh

  The fixture is three sections, fail-fast across them:
  - pristine-lifecycle-gate: case 1 (rbtdrp_marshal_zero_attestation) — pure local file inspection of working tree, RBRR blanks,
  RBRA credentials absent, nameplate hallmarks blank, vessel rbrv depot-fields blank. If this fails, cases 2-3 don't run.
  - pristine-lifecycle-simple: cases 2-3 — real GCP, levy a depot per case, list, unmake, list.

  Expected first-run monikers (no prior pristq/pristg depots):
  - Case 2 (depot-lifecycle):   prlc-d-pristq100000
  - Case 3 (governor-lifecycle): prlc-d-pristg100000

  Family-stem autodetect (rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker) consumes <moniker>.depot fact files emitted by the pre-levy depot_list
  invocation. First run sees an empty namespace, falls back to floor (100000); subsequent runs increment.

  Failure-mode contract per paddock: stop on first failure cleanly. Recovery is rbw-MZ + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. Mid-fixture
   failures may leave orphan GCP projects that lifecycle out via 30-day grace; if the soft-delete graveyard interferes with
  re-runs, the moniker autodetect picks past the collision.

  Things to watch for:
  - Marshal-zero attestation fails → fixture short-circuits before any GCP call. Read the diagnostic; the most common cause is
  forgetting to commit after rbw-MZ.
  - A levy that times out at IAM propagation polls means the depot project was created but not fully provisioned. The project still
   exists in GCP and counts against quota until soft-deleted.
  - Rerunning the fixture without rbw-MZ first will fail attestation; that's the design.

  Out-of-scope and known-stale: RBSDE/RBSDU/RBSDL operation specs still describe pre-collapse shapes. BBAAK cases 4-5 are a
  separate follow-on pace per Step 1 paddock locks. Do not wrap ₢BBAAM until live verification of cases 2-3 passes.

  Mount the pace, walk through the prerequisite chain with the operator, then run the fixture. Surface failures verbatim — the
  runbook says debug on the failed branch immediately.

  Tighten or expand to taste. The "things to watch for" block is the part most prone to bit-rot — feel free to drop it if you find
  it noisy.

Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Foundational simplification. Collapses depot identity in RBRR to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names, displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than hidden behind a timestamp.
  
  Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - RBRR field collapse: drop RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM, replaced by RBDC-derived constants computed at kindle.
  - Reshape rbgp_depot_levy: drop CLI positional arg, drop timestamp; project_id and sub-resource names come from RBDC kindle; trim post-levy banner to confirmation only.
  - Reshape rbgp_depot_unmake: drop CLI positional arg; target derives from RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER through RBDC.
  - Remove differential-furnish skip in rbgp_cli.sh so levy goes through zrbrr_enforce like every other depot operation.
  - Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices beyond intent.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation.
  - 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call at implementation.

  ## Character
  
  Foundational simplification. Collapses depot identity in RBRR to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out of those two at kindle.   - Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices beyond intent.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation.
  - 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call at implementation.

content.content:
1	## Context
2	
3	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
4	
5	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
6	
7	## Single operator prerequisite chain
8	
9	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
10	
11	## Entry contract — load-bearing
12	
13	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
14	
15	- Working tree clean
16	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
17	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
18	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
19	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
20	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
21	
22	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
23	
24	## Failure mode contract
25	
26	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
27	
28	## Release-branch execution contract
29	
30	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
31	
32	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
33	
34	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
35	
36	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
37	
38	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
39	
40	## Tier layering
41	
42	Three tiers, escalating cost:
43	
44	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
45	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
46	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
47	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
48	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
49	
50	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
51	
52	## Operator scope
53	
54	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
55	
56	## Coupling to ₣A_
57	
58	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
59	
60	## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)
61	
62	These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
63	
64	**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.
65	
66	**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.
67	
68	**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:
69	
70	- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
71	- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
72	- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
73	- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`
74	
75	**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.
76	
77	**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.
78	
79	**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.
80	
81	**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).
82	
83	## References
84	
85	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
86	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
87	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
88	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

## Context

Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

Worst-case math under existing individual caps: CLOUD_PREFIX max 11 (incl. trailing hyphen) + 'd-'(2) + MONIKER max 26 = 39 chars; the joint cap excludes the 30..39 sliver from regime validity. Mounted. Posting the orientation so we can decide the first move together.

## Heat / Pace
- Heat: ₣BB rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification — racing, 5/17 done
- Pace: ₢BBAAM rbrr-depot-identity-collapse — last of 7 steps just landed (Step 5 commit `d2dec554`), all 7 paddock locks aligned
- Tree: clean. HEAD is `4241cf70` (this session's OFFICIUM commit on top of `33ff7de0` jjx_redocket on top of `d2dec554` Step 5)

## Surfacing a contract violation before we move
Paddock §"Release-branch execution contract" says: **Run on a release branch. The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main.** We are on `main`. The contract reasoning is load-bearing: a failed run leaves bygone commits as history, and `rbw-MZ` doesn't touch them — that's only safe on a branch you can leave behind.

I will not run pristine-lifecycle on `main`. Two coherent options:

1. **Cut a release branch first** — e.g. `release/BBAAM-pristine-1`. Marshal-zero + commit + run happen there. If it fails, the branch is the debugging surface; if it passes, the trail is decoupled from main until you decide what to do with it.
2. **You already have a release-branch convention I'm missing** — point me at it and I'll switch.

## Prerequisite chain (paddock-mandated, in order)
Once we're on a release branch:

1. **Payor OAuth health** — `tt/rbw-gPR.PayorRefresh.sh` if needed. This is the *only* credential the operator brings; everything else is minted by the qualification.
2. **Marshal-zero** — `tt/rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh` — blanks RBRR, deletes RBRA files, blanks nameplate hallmarks, blanks vessel rbrv depot fields.
3. **Commit the marshal-zero state** — case 1 (`rbtdrp_marshal_zero_attestation`) inspects the HEAD commit's signature; uncommitted marshal-zero fails attestation and short-circuits cases 2-3.
4. **Run the fixture** — `tt/rbtd-r.Run.pristine-lifecycle.sh`. Three sections, fail-fast across them: `pristine-lifecycle-gate` (case 1, local), `pristine-lifecycle-simple` (cases 2-3, real GCP).

## What we expect
- Case 2 first-run moniker: `prlc-d-pristq100000`
- Case 3 first-run moniker: `prlc-d-pristg100000`
- Each case: levy → list (assert present) → unmake → list (assert absent or DELETE_REQUESTED). Real cloud spend, ~minutes per case.

## Question
**Do you want me to cut a release branch (and what name), or are you running this differently?**

I haven't touched anything yet — pure read.

# Release Procedure

The release qualification ceremony for the project maintainer — five operator steps, roughly one hour wall-clock, with cloud cost on the order of two GCP projects per run.
The ceremony exists to catch silent first-build assumptions that the routine `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` and `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` tiers tolerate by design.

[Payor](README.md#Payor) OAuth is the only prerequisite credential.
All other credentials — [Governor](README.md#Governor), [Director](README.md#Director), and [Retriever](README.md#Retriever) service-account [RBRA](README.md#RBRA) files — are minted by the qualification itself, not restored from backup.
The entire credential chain must be reproducible from a clean local state.

**Failure mode contract.** Mid-qualification failure means start over from step 2, not patch-and-continue.
Patching forward is exactly the bug class this ceremony exists to catch — accumulated state hides first-build assumptions.

## 1. Confirm Payor health

- [Refreshes](README.md#Refresh) the [Payor](README.md#Payor) OAuth token so subsequent [Payor](README.md#Payor)-authority operations do not fail mid-qualification on token expiry
- Use `tt/rbw-gPI.PayorInstall.sh` instead if [Payor](README.md#Payor) credentials have never been [Installed](README.md#Install) on this workstation
- Success: the command exits clean
- Failure: re-[Establish](README.md#Establish) the [Manor](README.md#Manor) before proceeding — the ceremony cannot run without a healthy [Payor](README.md#Payor)

```
tt/rbw-gPR.PayorRefresh.sh
```

## 2. Marshal zero, then commit

The marshal-zero operation returns local state to a blank template:

- Blanks [Repo Regime](README.md#RBRR) fields ([Depot](README.md#Depot) identity, project ID, prefixes)
- Deletes all [RBRA](README.md#RBRA) credential files ([Governor](README.md#Governor), [Director](README.md#Director), [Retriever](README.md#Retriever))
- Blanks [Hallmark](README.md#Hallmark) pins in each [Nameplate](README.md#Nameplate) regime file ([RBRN](README.md#RBRN))
- Blanks depot-scoped fields in each [Vessel](README.md#Vessel) regime file ([RBRV](README.md#RBRV))

Then commit the result with the marshal-zero signature in the commit message — step 3 detects that signature on `HEAD` and refuses to run otherwise.

- Success: working tree clean and `HEAD` carries the signature
- Failure: review the marshal output for files the operation would not zero, resolve, and retry

```
tt/rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh
```

## 3. Run the pristine qualification

The release gate.

**Entry contract** — `rbw-tP` refuses to run unless ALL of the following hold:

- marshal-zero signature on `HEAD`
- working tree clean
- [RBRA](README.md#RBRA) files absent
- [Repo Regime](README.md#RBRR) fields blank
- [Hallmark](README.md#Hallmark) pins blank in every [Nameplate](README.md#Nameplate) regime ([RBRN](README.md#RBRN))
- depot-scoped fields blank in every [Vessel](README.md#Vessel) regime file ([RBRV](README.md#RBRV))

The contract is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline — that property is what lets this tier catch silent-first-build assumptions by construction.

**Cost** — ~1 hour wall-clock plus two GCP projects per run:

- one canonical [Depot](README.md#Depot) [Levied](README.md#Levy) by the canonical-infrastructure setup phase
- GCP project deletion is asynchronous (30-day soft delete) — each run leaves pending-delete projects in the project list; this is accepted cost

**Sequence**:

- `pristine-lifecycle` fixture — marshal-zero gate plus idempotent throwaway lifecycles for [Depot](README.md#Depot), [Governor](README.md#Governor), [Retriever](README.md#Retriever), and [Director](README.md#Director)
- canonical infrastructure setup — [Mantle](README.md#Mantle) the [Governor](README.md#Governor) + deploy [RBRA](README.md#RBRA), [Charter](README.md#Charter) the [Retriever](README.md#Retriever) + deploy [RBRA](README.md#RBRA), [Knight](README.md#Knight) the [Director](README.md#Director) + deploy [RBRA](README.md#RBRA), [Levy](README.md#Levy) the canonical [Depot](README.md#Depot)
- supply chain — inscribe [Reliquary](README.md#Reliquary), [Ordain](README.md#Ordain) all [Hallmarks](README.md#Hallmark)
- [Crucible](README.md#Crucible) suite
- cleanup

Outcome:

- Success: green tally and clean exit
- Failure: return to step 2 — never patch-and-continue

```
tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh
```

## 4. Prepare the upstream release

- Claude Code slash command, not a tabtarget — the contribution ceremony is interactive by design
- Drives the upstream contribution review and produces the contribution-ready branch
- Success: the ceremony reports a clean upstream-ready state
- Failure: address findings and re-run; if the underlying issue is structural, return to step 2

```
/rbk-prep-release
```

## 5. Push to the upstream remote

- Universal git operation; no tabtarget
- Pushes the contribution-ready branch produced by step 4
- Success: the upstream accepts the push
- Failure: resolve the upstream-side reason (branch protection, force-push policy, etc.) and retry — the local state from step 4 remains valid

```
git push <upstream> <branch>
```

Three divergences.

**Divergence 1 — `rbw-tP` doesn't exist yet.** RELEASE.md step 3 says `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`, and that wraps a sequence: pristine-lifecycle fixture → canonical infrastructure setup → supply chain → Crucible suite → cleanup. We have only `rbw-tf` and `rbw-tr` today; `rbw-tP` is the target the paddock is building toward. The fixture-direct-run path your mount text used (`tt/rbtd-r.Run.pristine-lifecycle.sh`) is one component of step 3, not the documented entry point.

**Divergence 2 — I skipped RELEASE.md step 1.** Step 1 is `tt/rbw-gPR.PayorRefresh.sh` — confirm Payor OAuth health *before* marshal-zero. The text reads: *"so subsequent Payor-authority operations do not fail mid-qualification on token expiry."* I went straight to step 2. That's a real gap.

**Divergence 3 — RELEASE.md is silent on the push.** The rblm_zero guard refusing on unpushed commits is a safety property baked into the implementation, but RELEASE.md doesn't mention pushing as a prerequisite to step 2. So whether to push to `main` is *outside* the documented procedure — it's a guard surfacing a question RELEASE.md doesn't answer.

## What's actually happening
We're debugging the just-landed Step 5 of BBAAM (the pristine-lifecycle fixture's case 2/3 reshape) against live GCP. That's narrower than RELEASE.md's full ceremony — appropriate, because:
- The full ceremony (`rbw-tP`) isn't built yet
- We need first-cycle confidence in the reshaped fixture before it gets wrapped into `rbw-tP`

## Proposed correction
1. **Do step 1 first** — `tt/rbw-gPR.PayorRefresh.sh` (cheap, idempotent, removes a mid-fixture failure mode)
2. **Then resolve the push question** — your call: push to `main`, or revisit the release-branch decision
3. **Then step 2 + commit + run the fixture** — narrowed to fixture only, since `rbw-tP` doesn't exist

content.text:
Three concerns, in load-bearing order.

**1. Inversion of debug order.** The mount text and paddock both scope ₢BBAAM's remaining work to *"exercise end-to-end against real GCP"* the just-landed Step 5. If we wrap the unverified fixture into `rbw-tP` first, we conflate two failure surfaces: the fixture's reshape (Step 5 internals) and the wrapper's gate-check + sequencing logic. A bug we'd catch cleanly in the fixture-direct-run becomes harder to diagnose inside the wrapper. Cheap to flip the order — fixture-direct-run is one tabtarget invocation; draft `rbw-tP` after.

**2. Drafting `rbw-tP` is a real pace, not a quick wrapper.** Per paddock, the entry contract is load-bearing — *"refuses to run unless ALL of the following hold: working tree clean, HEAD is marshal-zero signed, RBRR blank, RBRA absent, hallmark pins blank, RBRV depot fields blank."* That's six gate checks plus the sequencing of fixture → canonical infra → supply chain → Crucible → cleanup. Per RELEASE.md, those phases each have load-bearing cost (~1hr + 2 GCP projects). This is a separate pace, possibly its own — looking at the heat I don't see a pace dedicated to building `rbw-tP` yet, so it would need to be enrolled. Calling it a "draft with currently available tests" papers over scope that the paddock has not yet curried.

**3. RELEASE.md step 1 still skipped.** Payor refresh hasn't run. Doing it costs nothing and removes a mid-run failure mode regardless of which path we take.

**Concern with the push, separately**: none. Pushing 35+ unpushed commits to `main` is fine — they're all affiliated heat work (₢BBAAM, ₢BBAAO, ₣BB) plus the OFFICIUM/jjx bookkeeping pattern this repo uses. The `rblm_zero` guard surfacing it as a precondition is doing exactly what BBAAB designed it to do.

## What I'd recommend
1. Push (need your nod — shared state)
2. RELEASE.md step 1 — Payor refresh
3. RELEASE.md step 2 — marshal-zero + commit
4. **Just the pristine-lifecycle fixture** (`tt/rbtd-r.Run.pristine-lifecycle.sh`) — verifies Step 5 cleanly
5. *Then*, as a follow-on pace, draft `rbw-tP` with the verified fixture + currently available tests

The `rbw-tP` draft is a worthy pace — I don't want to talk you out of it. I just want it after we know Step 5 stands on its own. **Want to flip to that order, or proceed as you proposed?**

content.text:
Spook surfaced. Diagnosis:

**Root cause**: Commit `4f1543cb` (₢A6AAe) added `zbuym_sentinel` call into `zbuh_kindle` and updated *some* consumers to also `source buym_yelp.sh`. Four consumers were missed in the sweep:
- `Tools/buk/buhw_cli.sh`
- `Tools/buk/burh_cli.sh`
- `Tools/rbk/rbgp_cli.sh`
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` ← marshal-zero

`rblm_cli.sh` sources `buh_handbook.sh` but not `buym_yelp.sh`, so when `zbuh_kindle` calls `zbuym_sentinel` the function is undefined → marshal-zero dies.

**Two fix shapes:**

**A. Per-consumer (matches established pattern)** — add `source buym_yelp.sh` before `source buh_handbook.sh` in each of the four missed consumers. Mechanical, conservative, matches the ₢A6AAe conversion intent.

**B. Self-sourcing in buh_handbook.sh** — have `buh_handbook.sh` source `buym_yelp.sh` itself. Eliminates the missing-kindle bug class entirely. Architecturally cleaner but breaks whatever BUK convention says modules don't transitively source.

**Pace affiliation question**: this regression is from ₣A6 territory (₢A6AAe), but it's blocking ₢BBAAM's verification. JJK additive-only discipline + multi-officium concerns: do I fix it inside ₢BBAAM, or capture as a separate spook pace?

I lean **A, inside ₢BBAAM as a notch**: matches established pattern, mechanical, unblocks BBAAM verification, the four-line fix is well within a pace's notch grain. ₢A6AAe is the historical pace and is closed; reopening it for an incomplete sweep doesn't fit the JJK closing-with-lessons model.

**Want me to apply Fix A and notch it into ₢BBAAM, or take a different shape?**

Got it — BUK convention is CLI-as-orchestrator, modules don't transitively source. So Fix A is the only correct shape, not just the conservative one. The four `_cli.sh` files (`buhw_cli`, `burh_cli`, `rbgp_cli`, `rblm_cli`) each need `source buym_yelp.sh` added before the `source buh_handbook.sh` line.

message.content: Are you clear you should _only_ be sourcing in a _cli.sh function?

content.text:
Yes, now I am — and I had it as soft convention rather than a rule. To be explicit: `source` statements appear *only* in `_cli.sh` files. Every other module (`buh_handbook.sh`, `buym_yelp.sh`, `buc_command.sh`, all the regime/utility modules) is library code with no `source` lines. The CLI is the single orchestration point declaring the load order; nothing else pulls dependencies. Option B (self-sourcing inside `buh_handbook.sh`) was architecturally forbidden, not just stylistically off.

Fix A is therefore the only shape: add `source buym_yelp.sh` before `source buh_handbook.sh` in each of the four `_cli.sh` files.

Awaiting your nod.

After enforce succeeds, lock all enrolled `PREFIX_*` variables with `readonly` to prevent accidental mutation by downstream code. Any derived state (e.g., docker env arrays) must be built from validated values after enforce, before or during the lock step.
108:**Singleton** regimes source config unconditionally in furnish. **Manifold** regimes (multiple instances, e.g. nameplates) source conditionally when a folio identifies the instance.

121:| `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Implementation | `test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" \|\| buc_die` | No                | No                | Guard all other functions                        |
122:| `z«prefix»_enforce`  | Implementation | `z«prefix»_sentinel`                               | No                | No                | Validate sourced config (regime archetype only)  |
123:| `z«prefix»_furnish`  | CLI only       | `buc_doc_env...`                                   | Yes (all deps)    | No                | Document env vars, source all deps, kindle       |

127:**Kindle constant**: Any variable — internal (`Z«PREFIX»_SCREAMING_NAME`) or public (`«PREFIX»_SCREAMING_NAME`) — defined exclusively within the kindle function with `readonly`. No other function — including enroll, setup, or helper functions — may assign to kindle constants. This ensures module state is fully determined at kindle time and visible in one place.
129:**Mutable kindle state**: A variable initialized in kindle but intentionally mutated after kindle returns (counters, accumulators, registry rolls, builder state). Uses **lowercase** `z_«prefix»_name` to visually distinguish from `readonly` kindle constants. Never apply `readonly` to mutable kindle state.
131:**Tinder constant**: A public variable (`«PREFIX»_lower_name`) defined at module top level, immediately after the `Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED=1` guard. Tinder constants must be pure string literals with **no variable expansion, no computation, and no runtime dependency**. They are available immediately after sourcing — no kindle required. Use `SCREAMING` case for the prefix (module identity) and `lower_snake` case for the name to visually distinguish from kindle constants. This enables sourcing chains where a tinder constant from module A provides the path for sourcing module B's config in the furnish function. The name reflects their role in the kindle metaphor: tinder is the fixed material prepared before kindling — inert, unchanging, and enabling the fire to start.

Since sentinel checks this variable, setting it last guarantees all kindle constants, roll arrays, and enroll calls are complete before the module is considered operational. rblm_zero() {
  buc_doc_brief "Zero regime to blank template for release qualification"

  git status --porcelain > "${z_status_temp}" || buc_die "git status failed"

  if ! git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name '@{u}' > "${z_upstream_temp}" 2>&1; then
    buc_die "Current branch has no upstream — set tracking and push before marshal-zero. See: ${z_upstream_temp}"
  fi

  git rev-list '@{u}..HEAD' > "${z_unpushed_temp}" || buc_die "git rev-list failed"
  test ! -s "${z_unpushed_temp}" || buc_die "HEAD has unpushed commits — push before marshal-zero. See: ${z_unpushed_temp}"

  # Discover secrets dir and vessel dir for pre-confirmation inventory
  local z_secrets_dir=""
  local z_vessel_dir=""

    case "${z_secrets_line}" in
      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*) z_secrets_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=}" ;;
      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)  z_vessel_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=}"  ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrr}"

  buh_line "  RBRR fields blanked (zeroed to onboarding start):"
  buh_line "    RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX,"
  buh_line "    RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER"

  buh_line "  RBRR fields pre-filled to defaults:"

  buh_line "    RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS, RBRR_GCP_REGION,"

  buh_e

    local z_preview=""
    local z_any_cred=0
    for z_preview in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"; then

    buh_line "    (secrets dir not configured)"
  fi
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Vessel hallmarks BLANKED (stale after depot change):"
  local z_np_preview=""
  local z_any_np=0
  for z_np_preview in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_np_preview}" || continue

  done

  buh_e
  buh_line "  Vessel regime fields BLANKED (depot-scoped, stale after depot change):"
  buh_line "    RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR in all rbrv.env"

    local z_vr_preview=""

      # Pre-selected defaults
      RBRR_DNS_SERVER=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_DNS_SERVER=8.8.8.8"                     ;;
      RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=*)              printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=e2-standard-2"         ;;
      RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=*)                   printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=2700s"                      ;;
      RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=*)     printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=3"            ;;
      RBRR_GCP_REGION=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCP_REGION=us-central1"                 ;;
      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=rbev-vessels"                ;;
      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*)                   printf '%s\n' "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=../station-files/secrets"   ;;
      # Site-specific fields blanked
      RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX=*)                  printf '%s\n' "RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX="                          ;;
      RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX=*)                printf '%s\n' "RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX="                        ;;
      RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=*)                 printf '%s\n' "RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER="                         ;;
      RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL=*)               continue                                                      ;;
      # Everything else passes through (comments, shebang, blanks)
      *)                                    printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                                   ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrr}" > "${z_tmp}" && mv "${z_tmp}" "${z_rbrr}"

  # Remove depot-scoped RBRA files (governor, director, retriever).
  # z_secrets_dir already extracted above for pre-confirmation inventory.
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
    local z_rbra=""
    for z_rbra in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}"; then
        rm "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}" || buc_die "Failed to remove: ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}"
        buh_line "  Removed stale depot credential: ${z_rbra}"
      fi
    done
  fi

  # Blank hallmark values in all vessel nameplates.
  # Hallmarks reference images built against the prior depot — they
  # become stale after reset.  Blanking them causes the onboarding guide
  # to require conjure & vouch before declaring setup complete.
  local z_np=""
  local z_np_tmp=""
  for z_np in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_np}" || continue
    z_np_tmp="${z_np}.tmp"
    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
      case "${z_line}" in
        RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=*)  printf '%s\n' "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=" ;;
        RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=*)  printf '%s\n' "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=" ;;
        *)                           printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                  ;;
      esac
    done < "${z_np}" > "${z_np_tmp}" && mv "${z_np_tmp}" "${z_np}"
    buh_line "  Blanked hallmarks: ${z_np}"
  done

  # Blank depot-scoped fields in all vessel regime files.
  # RBRV_RELIQUARY references a reliquary inscribed to the prior depot's GAR.
  # RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR references enshrined base images in the prior depot's GAR.
  # Both become stale after depot change — onboarding requires inscribe + enshrine.
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_vr=""
    local z_vr_tmp=""
    for z_vr in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_vr}" || continue
      z_vr_tmp="${z_vr}.tmp"
      while IFS= read -r z_line; do
        case "${z_line}" in
          RBRV_RELIQUARY=*)       printf '%s\n' "RBRV_RELIQUARY="       ;;
          RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR=*)  printf '%s\n' "${z_line%%=*}="        ;;
          *)                      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"             ;;
        esac
      done < "${z_vr}" > "${z_vr_tmp}" && mv "${z_vr_tmp}" "${z_vr}"
      buh_line "  Blanked depot-scoped fields: ${z_vr}"
    done
  fi

  buh_line "  Zero complete: ${z_rbrr}"
  buh_e

  # Auto-commit the in-tree mutations so post-zero state is captured as a single
  # commit. RBRA deletions (outside repo) are not staged.
  buc_step "Committing marshal-zero state"
  git add "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "Failed to stage RBRR file"

  local z_stage=""
  for z_stage in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
    git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
  done
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    for z_stage in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
      git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
    done
  fi

  if git diff --cached --quiet; then
    buh_line "  No changes to commit — already in marshal-zero state"
  else
    git commit -m "Marshal Zero — release qualification reset" || buc_die "Marshal-zero commit failed"
    buh_line "  Marshal-zero state committed"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_line "  Next: verify onboarding guide detects blank state:"
  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ONBOARDING}"
  buc_success "Regime zeroed to blank template"
}
